steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rearrange $3x - 7y \gt 21$ to see which region satisfies it: divide by $-7$ and flip the inequality to get $y \lt \frac{3}{7}x - 3$. The solution set is strictly below the line $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$, which has a positive slope and crosses the $y$-axis at $-3$. The question asks which region contains NO solutions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \gt 0$ (Quadrant II): test $(-1, 1)$: ${3}(-1) - 7(1) = -10$, which is not $\gt 21$. Try any point with $x \lt 0$, $y \gt 0$: the line $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$ is below $-3$ whenever $x \lt 0$, so all of Quadrant II lies above the boundary. No solutions exist here. ✓
  • Choice B — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \lt 0$ (Quadrant III): test $(-1, -10)$: ${3}(-1) - 7(-10) = 67 \gt 21$. Solutions exist here. ✗
  • Choice C — The region where $x \gt 0$ and $y \gt 0$ (Quadrant I): test $(100, 1)$: ${3}(100) - 7(1) = 293 \gt 21$. Solutions exist here. ✗
  • Choice D — The $x$-axis (where $y = 0$): $3x \gt 21$ gives $x \gt 7$, so points like $(8, 0)$ are solutions. Solutions exist on the positive $x$-axis. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
